title: Java User Input
content:
### User Input in Java

The `Scanner` class is used to read user input from the console.

---

### Import Scanner

```java
import java.util.Scanner;
```

### Creating Scanner Object

```java
Scanner scanner = new Scanner(System.in);
```

---

### Scanner Methods

| Method | Reads |
|--------|-------|
| `nextLine()` | String (entire line) |
| `next()` | String (single word) |
| `nextInt()` | Integer |
| `nextDouble()` | Double |
| `nextBoolean()` | Boolean |
| `nextByte()` | Byte |
| `nextFloat()` | Float |
| `nextLong()` | Long |
| `nextShort()` | Short |

---

### Reading Different Types

```java
Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);

System.out.print("Enter name: ");
String name = sc.nextLine();

System.out.print("Enter age: ");
int age = sc.nextInt();

System.out.print("Enter salary: ");
double salary = sc.nextDouble();
```

---

### Closing Scanner

Always close the scanner when done:

```java
scanner.close();
```

> **Key Point:** `nextInt()`, `nextDouble()` etc. leave the newline character in the buffer. If you call `nextLine()` after them, it consumes the leftover newline. Use an extra `nextLine()` to consume it first.
